response.justification: Candidate is an accomplished full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship habits, but the resume does not show the core requirements for this embedded-security role. There is no evidence of native Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel modification work, no embedded networking security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), and no L2/L3 switch or chip-vendor SDK exposure. C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments roughly a decade ago; the bulk of recent work is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang on web platforms.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software development with strong testing/TDD discipline
Some prior C/C++ experience at National Instruments on a UNIX-adjacent IDE product
Exposure to Linux environments, Docker, CI/CD, and low-latency systems work
concerns: No indication of native-level Japanese language proficiency (hard requirement)
No demonstrated embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or networking-security experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3)
No Linux kernel source modification or chip-vendor SDK work shown; recent stack is web/cloud, not embedded C
